definition: A celestial body that orbits the Sun, is round due to its own gravity, but has not cleared its orbital neighborhood of other debris, per the 2006 IAU definition.
source_locations: dwarf-planets.md (all sections); solar-system-overview.md (Solar System Facts, 10 Things #1, Planets)

definition: Popular nickname for Mars, referencing its reddish surface color.
usage_context: informal/narrative references to Mars used in place of repeating the word Mars
source_locations: mars.md (Mars Overview, Why Do We Go?, Perseverance/Curiosity section, Humans to Mars)

part_of_speech: noun (common); "Moon" as proper noun for Earth's
definition: A natural body that orbits a planet or dwarf planet. Capitalized "Moon" refers specifically to Earth's moon; lowercase "moon"/"moons" refers to the general class of such bodies orbiting any planet.
usage_context: planetary science descriptions of orbiting bodies
forbidden_variants: satellite (ambiguous with artificial/man-made satellites; reserve for direct size comparisons only, as with Pluto-Charon)
source_locations: solar-system-overview.md (Solar System Facts, Moons, 10 Things #9); mars.md (Humans to Mars); dwarf-planets.md (Pluto: The Star of Dwarf Planets, Why Is Pluto No Longer a Planet?)

definition: The star at the center of our solar system, around which the eight planets and five dwarf planets orbit.
usage_context: astronomy docs referring specifically to our solar system's star
forbidden_variants: host star (acceptable only in general/abstract planet-definition statements not specific to our system)
source_locations: dwarf-planets.md (Dwarf Planets Overview, intro); solar-system-overview.md (Solar System Facts)

definition: The international body of professional astronomers that formally defines and classifies astronomical objects, including the 2006 planet/dwarf-planet distinction.
source_locations: dwarf-planets.md (Dwarf Planets Overview, Why Is Pluto No Longer a Planet?)

definition: An uncrewed vehicle used to explore a planet. "Rover" moves on the surface; "orbiter" circles the planet without landing; "spacecraft" is the umbrella term covering both; "mission" refers to the overall program, not the hardware itself.
forbidden_variants: mission (when used to mean the vehicle itself rather than the program)
source_locations: mars.md (Active Missions); dwarf-planets.md (Pluto: The Star of Dwarf Planets); solar-system-overview.md (10 Things #9)

definition: The minor spiral arm of the Milky Way galaxy containing our solar system.
usage_context: descriptions of the solar system's location within the Milky Way
forbidden_variants: Orion Spur (presented in-source as an interchangeable alternate name; pick one as canonical)

definition: The Sun and all celestial bodies gravitationally bound to it, including planets, dwarf planets, moons, asteroids, and comets.
usage_context: base term used throughout for our planetary system
